summary:
Elastomerics range from poor (\(polysulfides\)) to excellent (\(addition siloxanes\)) dimensional stability. Condensation silicone and polyether fall between these extremes based on their respective mechanisms of polymerization.

The summary captures the essential information about how different types of elastomers rank according to their dimensional stability, focusing directly on what was asked regarding ordering them correctly. To improve conciseness further:
